title: Nimzo-Indian Defense: Sämisch Variation
summary: Master the Sämisch where White allows the pin and accepts doubled pawns for central control.
description: The Sämisch Variation is one of White's most aggressive tries against the Nimzo-Indian. By playing f3, White allows Black to double the f-pawns but gains central control and king safety. This tutorial covers the main line and typical plans.

variations.modern: 6.f3 - The Sämisch with early f3, allowing ...Nxe4
variations.avoidance: 4.f3 - The Sämisch Gambit, avoiding the pin entirely

objectives: Understand the Sämisch structure: doubled f-pawns but central control
Learn why White allows the pin: development and king safety
Master typical plans: e4 break for White, ...d5 for Black
plans: White's plan: Push e4 to attack, use the space advantage
Black's plan: Play ...d5, ...cxd4, and activate the pieces
The move ...cxd4 is a key exchange, opening the position
White should avoid exchanges - keep pieces for the attack
commonMistakes: Do not think doubled pawns are always bad - they can be a strength
Avoid playing too passively - White has the initiative
Do not underestimate Black's activity - the bishop on b4 is powerful
White should push e4 at the right moment to open the position
completionTitle: Complex position with chances for both
completionSummary: The Sämisch Variation is a dynamic line. You've learned the main ideas and can play both sides.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: The Nimzo-Indian is one of Black's most strategic defenses.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Black attacks d4 and prepares the Nimzo-Indian.

steps.title: Challenge the center
steps.coaching: This is the standard setup against the Nimzo-Indian.

steps.title: Black plays e6
steps.instruction: Black plays e6, preparing the Nimzo-Indian.
steps.coaching: Black is setting up the Nimzo-Indian structure.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, controlling d5 and e4.

steps.title: The Nimzowitsch pin
steps.instruction: The bishop pins the knight on c3.
steps.coaching: This is the Nimzo-Indian! Black pins and threatens ...Nxc3.

steps.title: The Sämisch move
steps.instruction: Play a3 to force the bishop to move.
steps.coaching: This is the Sämisch! White forces the bishop to capture.

steps.title: Black captures
steps.instruction: Black captures on c3, doubling White's pawns.
steps.coaching: Black accepts the Sämisch, doubling White's f-pawns.

steps.title: White recaptures
steps.coaching: White accepts the doubled pawns but gains central control.
steps.hint: Capture on c3 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black challenges the center
steps.coaching: This is Black's typical break against the Sämisch.

steps.title: Support the center
steps.instruction: Play e3 to support d4 and prepare development.
steps.coaching: White strengthens the center and prepares development.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.coaching: Black gets the king to safety and prepares piece development.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, controlling e5.

steps.title: Black prepares development
steps.instruction: Black plays b6, preparing the bishop development.
steps.coaching: Black is setting up the Queen's Indian structure.
